concerns.text: Task T019b states 'ensure gradients flow... to the solver parameters' but omits the critical requirement from FR-003 to verify gradients flow *through the GFM decoder*. FR-003 mandates: 'verify differentiability by ensuring gradients flow from the constraint violation loss, through the decoder, to the solver parameters'. Omitting the decoder path violates the core hypothesis of decoupling symbolic logic from decoder fidelity.

Task T031 sets a target to 'Reduce solver latency to <300ms/step'. This silently relaxes the spec's Assumption (and Plan) which defines the constraint as '<300 seconds per step'. Enforcing a 300ms target (1000x stricter) without explicit spec authorization constitutes a scope change that may render the 6-hour CI window impossible to meet if the original assumption was the only viable path.

Task T021b implements logic to measure 'one-second collision-free duration' but fails to include the 'target zone' verification required by SC-001. SC-001 defines success as 'reaching the target zone (center within 5cm) without collision for a duration of approximately one second'. Measuring only collision-free duration ignores the manipulation completion criteria, weakening the acceptance criterion.

Task T022a implements the baseline GAM script but lacks explicit constraints to 'freeze GFM weights' and 'run on CPU-only hardware'. SC-002 requires the baseline be measured on the 'same hardware configuration'. Without these explicit task constraints, the baseline could be run on GPU or with different precision, violating the comparative validity of the experiment.
